question: Please create a short deep research report on Nvidia's cuda-x and how the different libraries relate to one another.
ground_truth: The agent routes to aiq-research, resolves AIQ_SERVER_URL, checks backend health, submits the user's exact query through the AI-Q research flow, polls any returned async deep research job until completion, fetches the final report, and presents the report with citations and source URLs intact. If no compatible backend is reachable, it asks whether the user already has a backend URL or wants aiq-deploy to start a local Skill backend.
expected_behavior: Routes to aiq-research, not aiq-deploy, for the initial request
Checks AIQ_SERVER_URL or the default localhost backend before research
Uses scripts/aiq.py rather than inventing unrelated API calls
Preserves the user's CUDA-X query
Handles deep_research_running job IDs by polling with research_poll
Fetches or presents the final report when the job completes
Does not truncate citations or source URLs

question: Use AI-Q to check whether the local research backend is healthy.
ground_truth: The agent uses aiq-research to call the helper health command against AIQ_SERVER_URL or http://localhost:8000. It reports the checked URL and concise health status. It does not start deployment unless the backend is unreachable and the user chooses local deployment.
expected_behavior: Routes to aiq-research
Runs scripts/aiq.py health
Uses AIQ_SERVER_URL when set, otherwise the localhost default
Reports the URL that was checked
Does not print unrelated environment variables or secrets

question: Use AI-Q to list the available async research agent types.
ground_truth: The agent uses aiq-research to call the helper agents command and returns the async agent JSON without truncating important keys. If the endpoint is missing or fails after health succeeds, it reports backend compatibility failure and offers aiq-deploy validation.
expected_behavior: Routes to aiq-research
Runs scripts/aiq.py agents
Preserves JSON keys such as agent_type and description
Reports compatibility failure if the backend is reachable but the async agents endpoint fails
Does not silently retry with unrelated endpoints

question: Use AI-Q at http://localhost:8100 to research what AI-Q Blueprint is used for.
ground_truth: The agent treats the supplied URL as the target AIQ_SERVER_URL for subsequent helper calls, checks health against that URL, then uses the research flow. It does not ignore the user-supplied backend URL and does not assume any hosted NVIDIA-internal endpoint.
expected_behavior: Routes to aiq-research
Uses http://localhost:8100 as AIQ_SERVER_URL for helper calls
Runs health before research
Does not default back to http://localhost:8000 unless the user changes the URL
Does not mention or use internal hosted endpoints

question: Resume polling AI-Q job 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc and fetch the report if it is done.
ground_truth: The agent uses aiq-research helper commands for an existing async job. It checks status, uses report when the job is already complete, or research_poll when the job is still running. It does not cancel the job just because polling output is stale.
expected_behavior: Routes to aiq-research
Uses scripts/aiq.py status, report, or research_poll for the given job ID
Treats completed or success status as complete
Fetches the report when available
Does not cancel a successful or still-running job without user request

question: Install AI-Q and start the browser UI.
ground_truth: This is an install and UI deployment request, not a research request. The research skill should stay silent and the deploy skill should ask or follow the appropriate UI deployment path.
expected_behavior: Does not route to aiq-research
Routes to aiq-deploy
Treats the request as UI deployment rather than research
Does not call scripts/aiq.py chat or research
